hints.text: Gen4 = **large white pickup truck w/ a front antenna**, often fully or partly visible

shared_with.note: Panama's truck is almost always fully blurred.

hints.text: Plates short, white w/ blue text → **light-blue tint** through the blur
Lush tropical: rolling hills, agricultural lowlands, **peaky volcanoes through the centre**, dense rainforests dotted around
Less colonial than Panama: **dark-red sheet-metal/plastic-tile roofs**, pastel concrete walls, low or boxy 2-storey houses — **heavily fenced & barred**
**Spanish** official (Brazil = Portuguese)
Stop signs say **'ALTO'**

shared_with.note: Panama's ALTO uses a thicker font.

shared_with.diff: White pickup → CR; grey roof-rack car → GT.

hints.text: **Red taxis** are the standard
**Long silver insulators** on poles

shared_with.note: Panama mixes many insulator types (short silver, long black, UFO, mushroom).
Brazil can have long silver insulators.

hints.text: Common poletop: even crossbar w/ **uneven insulators (2-on-1)**, crossbar set lower than usual, usually **with support bars**; also single side-insulator at the very top; thin **L-bent metal crossbars**

shared_with.note: 2-on-1 low crossbars exist there, but usually WITHOUT support bars — and crossbars are rarer overall.

hints.text: Pole lamps w/ a **blue dot** on top (also PA/CO/BR)
Road signs often have a **cross on the back** (esp. diamond warnings)

shared_with.note: Colombian signs almost always have back crosses — but on WHITE signposts.
shared_with.diff: Grey posts → CR; white posts → CO.

hints.text: 'Bollard' = vertical **orange board w/ 3 black circles**
Electricity counters **circular, often w/ a big metal box** beside them (circular also in Mexico etc.)
**Imperial beer ads** w/ the black eagle mascot everywhere
**Provinces** appear on signs + have their own flags; area codes moderately useful
**Pole-marking metas**:
bullets: Large black/white pole stickers: first digit of the top number = province
Asymmetric poletops (1+2 insulators): in/around San José (fully one-sided: countrywide)
Wire-formed numbers on poles: central Alajuela + Heredia
White pole plates w/ black letters (metal bands): Greater Metropolitan Area
QR-code stickers: around Corralillo, south of San José
Yellow paint, tall w/ 3 numbered sections: Puntarenas + S-Alajuela
Yellow paint, short, no dividers: Guanacaste + NW-Alajuela
Yellow paint, 2 sections + horizontal characters below: almost only S-Alajuela
Black sticker w/ yellow letters near pole base: W-Guanacaste + Nicoya

hints.text: **Landscape & crops**:
bullets: Caribbean Lowlands (north): flat farmland, grey dirt, straight roads, often overcast
West: coverage mostly from the dry season
Bananas: Limón · Oil palms: coastal Puntarenas (+ some Limón)
Pineapple: W-Limón, N-Alajuela, Buenos Aires · Sugarcane: several areas (blooming silvery tufts → San Isidro/Buenos Aires)
Rice paddies: south of Bagaces (Guanacaste) · Coffee: central valley · Cassava: W-Limón
Teak (straight trunks, glossy round leaves): Guanacaste
Attalea palms: southern coast · Macaw palms (spiky trunk, messy round crown): west
Dark volcanic rocks: central CR, esp. Guapiles + Miravalles

hints.text: Taxis carry **yellow triangles w/ town name + province letter code**; driftwood-branch fences → west; irrigation canals → south of Bagaces/Cañas; stilt houses → NE coast; folded-down car antenna → north/NE
**San José**: biggest urban area, valley w/ inclined roads, taller buildings; municipality-wide **green street signs w/ white emblem rectangle** — Calle/Avenida numbers increase outward from downtown

**Road spotlights**: few train tracks (GMA → Puntarenas west; via Turrialba → Siquirres/Limón east); only concrete highways = eastern RN 18 + divided RN 1 Liberia↔RN 18; RN 32 (RN 4→Limón) = divided through flat lush lowlands, w/ **yellow centre bollards** between toll & RN 4; yellow follow-car w/ 2 front windows → RN 2 east of Palmar Norte; wind turbines → Guanacaste clusters + SW of Cartago; bright-white soil roads → north of Liberia; night coverage → San Vito; tuk-tuks → Puerto Viejo de Talamanca (+ Nosara)

**Rainbolt coverage**: Rainbolt / film crew on a black Toyota around **La Fortuna**; his Gen4 trekker (brown hair, camera crew, stair trail → waterfall) SW of La Fortuna; another tropical-trail trekker (black-haired) in Mistico Park west of La Fortuna

**Gen3 = trekkers only**: around San José + NW coast beaches, Cocos Island (500 km offshore) + Caño Island boat. Pinpointable ones incl. Children's Museum (castle look), National Museum tower, orange bridge at Parque de la Paz, La Sabana/National Stadium, University of CR, Palacio de los Deportes (Heredia), airport walk, 3 dogs in pine forest (Bosque de la Hoja), Cartago museum/ruins/stadium, Punta Leona resort (Turkish-style red bricks), Liberia white building, Filadelfia L-shaped bricks
